metadata.title: The Dollar Canon — AI+1 series 2/4
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: Portuguese short form (HAI budget) — HAI takes only the shortest forms (Hi · Ola · Hej · Ciao); never spell out HumanitariansAI in the greeting (the footer chip does that). Wagwan n/a (Bear only).
metadata.purpose: CUSTOMER pitch, not investor pitch: sell custom workshops, custom courses, and $1 Kindle books from a nonprofit doing good, and send viewers to the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat carries a short serif spark line. HANDOFF LAW: 2nd-to-last beat is the composer with a paste-ready action, greeting 'Your turn.'. OUTRO LAW: restate the title, poster serif, terracotta period. House grammar: >=1 PREDICT beat; verdict page carries the phase gate; decisions.json + DECISIONS.md ship beside this sheet.

beats.narration_text: … this is Liam, in for Bear. The question on the table is whether the textbook is really a dollar — and if so, why? The answer is yes, on purpose, and the business logic behind it is actually clarifying about how Humanitarians AI thinks about trust.

props.command: is the textbook really a dollar?

beats.narration_text: One dollar on Kindle — free if you have Kindle Unlimited. Every course, not a rotating selection. And it is the full canonical textbook: every chapter, every concept, every worked example. Not a preview chapter, not a content teaser designed to push you toward a paid upsell.

props.heading: One dollar on Kindle — free if you have Kindle Unlimited.
props.body: Every course, not a rotating selection. And it is the full canonical textbook: every chapter, every concept, every worked example. Not a preview chapter, not a content teaser designed to push you toward a paid upsell.

beats.narration_text: The lectures are free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube — the complete video course, public, no paywall. You can assign the whole course to a class, watch it yourself over a week, or use it to evaluate their teaching before you spend a single dollar on anything. The full course is the demo.

props.heading: The lectures are free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube — the complete video cours
props.body: You can assign the whole course to a class, watch it yourself over a week, or use it to evaluate their teaching before you spend a single dollar on anything. The full course is the demo.

beats.narration_text: The slide decks run on their site as voiced, animated pages. Every deck is narrated, every build is animated, and it all runs in the browser. No download, no license key, no viewer software to install. A teacher with a projector can walk their class through any deck using the site alone.

props.heading: The slide decks run on their site as voiced, animated pages.
props.body: Every deck is narrated, every build is animated, and it all runs in the browser. No download, no license key, no viewer software to install. A teacher with a projector can walk their class through any deck using the site alone.

beats.narration_text: Here's a predict beat worth sitting with. If the canonical course is free — video plus book plus decks — why price the Kindle book at all? And why would a nonprofit use price as a signal when reach is supposedly what matters? Commit to your hypothesis.

props.heading: Here's a predict beat worth sitting with.
props.body: If the canonical course is free — video plus book plus decks — why price the Kindle book at all? And why would a nonprofit use price as a signal when reach is supposedly what matters? Commit to your hypothesis.

beats.narration_text: Because the canon is the audit. Pricing it at a dollar keeps it visible in the Kindle catalog — a free book disappears into the noise. More importantly, you can read the book, watch the course, and hear every deck before you ever email about a custom version. When you do email, you know exactly what the base is. The canon earns the custom conversation.

props.heading: Because the canon is the audit.
props.body: Pricing it at a dollar keeps it visible in the Kindle catalog — a free book disappears into the noise. More importantly, you can read the book, watch the course, and hear every deck before you ever email about a custom version. When you do email, you know exactly what the base is. The canon earns the custom conversation.

beats.narration_text: Summary in one frame. A dollar gets you: the full canonical textbook, the complete video course on YouTube, and every slide deck voiced and animated on site. Nothing is behind a paywall except the Kindle file — and that's a dollar. The logic: audit the canon before you ever pay for a version of it.

artifactLines: $1 Kindle textbook (free on Kindle Unlimited) — the full canonical text, every course.
The complete course in video, free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
Every slide deck voiced and animated on the site — nothing to install.
Phase gate: audit the canon first · buy custom only when the base has earned it.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — audit them. The handoff prompt picks a course and checks the book against the videos: does the chapter coverage match the lecture coverage? If the canon holds up under that check, the custom conversation is worth your time.

props.command: Pick one Humanitarians AI course. Pull its $1 Kindle textbook and its free YouTube playlist, and check the chapters against the videos — does the coverage match?

beats.narration_text: The dollar canon. Next: your version — how you get a custom build you own the rights to transform. Liam, in for Bear.
